title: carex tenera var. invisa
intro: Carex tenera var. invisa, also known as false cattail or invasive sedgemallow, is a perennial sedge that belongs to the family Cyperaceae and is native to North America. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Carex tenera var. invisa are thought to include relief from skin irritations and wounds, as well as the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant is typically prepared as a decoction or infusion, and may also be used in salves or ointments to enhance its topical healing properties.
